description: Visualizes a single-qubit Bloch sphere by showing the |0⟩, |1⟩, and superposition states as labeled vectors on a unit sphere, illustrating the geometric meaning of qubit states.
code:
class BlochSphereScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Qubit States on the Bloch Sphere", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        # Draw the sphere outline as two ellipses
        sphere = Circle(radius=2.0, color=BLUE_C, stroke_width=2)
        sphere.move_to(ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.4)
        equator = Ellipse(width=4.0, height=1.2, color=BLUE_D, stroke_width=1.5)
        equator.move_to(sphere.get_center())
        self.play(Create(sphere), Create(equator))

        center = sphere.get_center()

        # |0> state (north pole)
        north = center + UP * 2.0
        v0 = Arrow(center, north, buff=0, color=GREEN, stroke_width=4)
        lbl0 = Text("|0⟩", font_size=26, color=GREEN).next_to(north, UP, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(v0), FadeIn(lbl0))
        self.wait(0.6)

        # |1> state (south pole)
        south = center + DOWN * 2.0
        v1 = Arrow(center, south, buff=0, color=RED, stroke_width=4)
        lbl1 = Text("|1⟩", font_size=26, color=RED).next_to(south, DOWN, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(v1), FadeIn(lbl1))
        self.wait(0.6)

        # Superposition state (equator, pointing right)
        eq_point = center + RIGHT * 2.0
        vplus = Arrow(center, eq_point, buff=0, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=4)
        lblplus = Text("|+⟩ = (|0⟩+|1⟩)/√2", font_size=22, color=YELLOW)
        lblplus.next_to(eq_point, RIGHT, buff=0.15)
        self.play(Create(vplus), FadeIn(lblplus))
        self.wait(0.6)

        # Z and X axis labels
        z_lbl = Text("Z", font_size=20, color=GREY_B).next_to(north, RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        x_lbl = Text("X", font_size=20, color=GREY_B).next_to(eq_point, DOWN, buff=0.1)
        self.play(FadeIn(z_lbl), FadeIn(x_lbl))

        note = Text("θ=0 → |0⟩, θ=π → |1⟩, θ=π/2 → |+⟩", font_size=18, color=GREY_B)
        note.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(FadeIn(note))
        self.wait(2.5)

description: Animates a 2-qubit quantum circuit performing a Bell state preparation, showing the Hadamard gate creating superposition followed by a CNOT gate creating entanglement.
code:
class BellStateCircuitScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Bell State Preparation Circuit", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        center = DOWN * 0.3
        # Draw two qubit wires
        y0 = center + UP * 0.8
        y1 = center + DOWN * 0.8
        wire0 = Line(LEFT * 5 + y0, RIGHT * 5 + y0, color=WHITE, stroke_width=2)
        wire1 = Line(LEFT * 5 + y1, RIGHT * 5 + y1, color=WHITE, stroke_width=2)
        qlbl0 = Text("|0⟩", font_size=24).next_to(wire0.get_left(), LEFT, buff=0.15)
        qlbl1 = Text("|0⟩", font_size=24).next_to(wire1.get_left(), LEFT, buff=0.15)
        self.play(Create(wire0), Create(wire1))
        self.play(FadeIn(qlbl0), FadeIn(qlbl1))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Hadamard gate on qubit 0
        h_box = Rectangle(width=0.8, height=0.8, color=BLUE, fill_color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.3)
        h_box.move_to(LEFT * 2 + y0)
        h_lbl = Text("H", font_size=24, color=WHITE).move_to(h_box)
        self.play(Create(h_box), FadeIn(h_lbl))
        cap1 = Text("Hadamard: |0⟩ → (|0⟩+|1⟩)/√2", font_size=20, color=YELLOW)
        cap1.to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.0)
        self.play(FadeIn(cap1))
        self.wait(1.5)
        self.play(FadeOut(cap1))

        # CNOT gate
        cnot_x = RIGHT * 1.0
        ctrl_dot = Dot(radius=0.12, color=BLUE).move_to(cnot_x + y0)
        cnot_line = Line(cnot_x + y0, cnot_x + y1, color=BLUE, stroke_width=2)
        target_circle = Circle(radius=0.3, color=BLUE, stroke_width=3).move_to(cnot_x + y1)
        cross_h = Line(LEFT * 0.3 + cnot_x + y1, RIGHT * 0.3 + cnot_x + y1, color=BLUE, stroke_width=2)
        cross_v = Line(DOWN * 0.3 + cnot_x + y1, UP * 0.3 + cnot_x + y1, color=BLUE, stroke_width=2)
        self.play(Create(ctrl_dot), Create(cnot_line))
        self.play(Create(target_circle), Create(cross_h), Create(cross_v))
        cap2 = Text("CNOT entangles qubits: output is Bell state |Φ⁺⟩", font_size=20, color=YELLOW)
        cap2.to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.0)
        self.play(FadeIn(cap2))
        self.wait(0.8)

        # Output state labels
        out0 = Text("(|00⟩+|11⟩)/√2", font_size=22, color=GREEN)
        out0.next_to(wire0.get_right(), RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        out1 = Text("← Bell state", font_size=20, color=GREEN)
        out1.next_to(wire1.get_right(), RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        self.play(FadeIn(out0), FadeIn(out1))
        self.wait(2.5)

description: Demonstrates the quantum phase kickback mechanism used in quantum phase estimation by showing how a controlled unitary transfers phase information from the target qubit to the control qubit.
code:
class PhaseKickbackScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Quantum Phase Kickback", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        center = ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.2

        # Step 1: Setup
        step1 = Text("Setup: control in |+⟩, target is eigenstate |u⟩", font_size=22, color=YELLOW)
        step1.move_to(center + UP * 1.8)
        self.play(FadeIn(step1))

        ctrl_lbl = Text("Control: |+⟩ = (|0⟩+|1⟩)/√2", font_size=20)
        ctrl_lbl.move_to(center + UP * 0.7)
        tgt_lbl = Text("Target: |u⟩ (eigenstate of U)", font_size=20)
        tgt_lbl.move_to(center)
        self.play(FadeIn(ctrl_lbl), FadeIn(tgt_lbl))
        self.wait(1.5)

        # Step 2: Apply controlled-U
        self.play(FadeOut(step1), FadeOut(ctrl_lbl), FadeOut(tgt_lbl))
        step2 = Text("Apply controlled-U", font_size=22, color=YELLOW)
        step2.move_to(center + UP * 1.8)
        action = Text("c-U (|0⟩+|1⟩)|u⟩ → |0⟩|u⟩ + e^ⁱφ|1⟩|u⟩", font_size=20)
        action.move_to(center + UP * 0.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(step2), FadeIn(action))
        self.wait(1.5)

        # Step 3: Phase kickback result
        self.play(FadeOut(step2), FadeOut(action))
        step3 = Text("Factor out: phase φ kicks back to control qubit", font_size=22, color=YELLOW)
        step3.move_to(center + UP * 1.8)
        result = Text("= (|0⟩ + e^ⁱφ|1⟩) ⊗ |u⟩", font_size=24, color=GREEN)
        result.move_to(center + UP * 0.4)
        arrow = Text("↑ phase φ is now encoded in the control qubit!", font_size=18, color=GREY_B)
        arrow.next_to(result, DOWN, buff=0.3)
        self.play(FadeIn(step3))
        self.play(Write(result))
        self.play(FadeIn(arrow))
        self.wait(1.5)

        # Step 4: Why it works
        self.play(FadeOut(step3), FadeOut(result), FadeOut(arrow))
        box = RoundedRectangle(width=8.5, height=1.4, corner_radius=0.2,
                               color=BLUE, fill_color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.15)
        box.move_to(center + UP * 0.3)
        key = Text("U|u⟩ = e^ⁱφ|u⟩  ⇒  eigenvalue phase φ is extracted", font_size=21)
        key.move_to(box)
        self.play(Create(box), FadeIn(key))
        use = Text("Used in: Shor's algorithm, quantum simulation", font_size=18, color=GREY_B)
        use.next_to(box, DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(FadeIn(use))
        self.wait(2.5)

description: Visualizes the gradient descent optimization algorithm by animating a ball rolling down a 1D loss curve, showing how the learning rate controls step size and convergence toward the minimum.
code:
class GradientDescentScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Gradient Descent Optimization", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-3.2, 3.2, 1],
            y_range=[-0.5, 9.5, 2],
            x_length=7.5,
            y_length=4.5,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_B},
        ).move_to(ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.4)
        x_lbl = Text("w (weight)", font_size=18, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.get_x_axis(), RIGHT, buff=0.15)
        y_lbl = Text("L(w)", font_size=18, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.get_y_axis(), UP, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(axes), FadeIn(x_lbl), FadeIn(y_lbl))

        loss_curve = axes.plot(lambda x: x**2, x_range=[-3, 3], color=BLUE_C, stroke_width=3)
        self.play(Create(loss_curve))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Animate gradient descent steps
        w = 2.8
        lr = 0.35
        dot = Dot(radius=0.14, color=YELLOW).move_to(axes.c2p(w, w**2))
        self.play(FadeIn(dot))

        for step in range(7):
            grad = 2 * w
            w_new = w - lr * grad
            new_pos = axes.c2p(w_new, w_new**2)
            step_lbl = Text(f"w ← w - α∇L  (step {step+1})", font_size=18, color=YELLOW)
            step_lbl.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)
            self.play(dot.animate.move_to(new_pos), FadeIn(step_lbl), run_time=0.6)
            self.play(FadeOut(step_lbl), run_time=0.2)
            w = w_new

        converge = Text("Converged near minimum w=0, L(w)=0", font_size=20, color=GREEN)
        converge.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(converge), Indicate(dot, scale_factor=2.0))
        self.wait(2.0)

description: Illustrates forward propagation through a 3-layer neural network by animating signal flow from input nodes through hidden layers to output, with weight labels on each connection.
code:
class NeuralNetworkForwardPassScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Neural Network: Forward Propagation", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        center = ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.3
        layer_x = [-4.0, -1.3, 1.3, 4.0]
        layer_sizes = [3, 4, 4, 2]
        layer_names = ["Input", "Hidden 1", "Hidden 2", "Output"]
        colors = [BLUE_C, TEAL, TEAL, ORANGE]

        all_nodes = []
        for l, (lx, sz, name, col) in enumerate(zip(layer_x, layer_sizes, layer_names, colors)):
            ys = [(sz - 1) / 2.0 * 0.9 - i * 0.9 for i in range(sz)]
            nodes = VGroup(*[Circle(radius=0.28, color=col, fill_color=col, fill_opacity=0.25)
                             .move_to(center + RIGHT * lx + UP * y) for y in ys])
            lbl = Text(name, font_size=16, color=GREY_B)
            lbl.next_to(nodes[0] if sz == 1 else nodes, UP, buff=0.2)
            all_nodes.append(nodes)
            self.play(LaggedStart(*[Create(n) for n in nodes], lag_ratio=0.1), FadeIn(lbl), run_time=0.5)

        # Draw connections between layers
        all_edges = []
        for l in range(len(layer_x) - 1):
            edges = VGroup()
            for n_a in all_nodes[l]:
                for n_b in all_nodes[l + 1]:
                    e = Line(n_a.get_right(), n_b.get_left(), stroke_width=1.0, color=GREY_C)
                    edges.add(e)
            all_edges.append(edges)
            self.play(FadeIn(edges), run_time=0.3)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Animate signal propagation layer by layer
        for l, edges in enumerate(all_edges):
            self.play(edges.animate.set_color(YELLOW).set_stroke(width=2.5), run_time=0.5)
            self.play(LaggedStart(*[Indicate(n, color=YELLOW) for n in all_nodes[l + 1]], lag_ratio=0.12), run_time=0.6)
            self.play(edges.animate.set_color(GREY_C).set_stroke(width=1.0), run_time=0.3)

        out_lbl = Text("得 output = softmax(W³ · σ(W² · σ(W¹x + b¹) + b²) + b³)", font_size=17, color=GREEN)
        out_lbl.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(FadeIn(out_lbl))
        self.wait(2.0)

description: Shows how a sigmoid activation function squashes any real-valued input into the range (0,1), plotting the curve and highlighting the saturation regions that cause the vanishing gradient problem.
code:
class SigmoidActivationScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Sigmoid Activation & Vanishing Gradients", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        import numpy as np

        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-6, 6, 2],
            y_range=[-0.1, 1.2, 0.5],
            x_length=8.0,
            y_length=3.8,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_B},
        ).move_to(ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.5)
        x_lbl = Text("z", font_size=20, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.get_x_axis(), RIGHT, buff=0.15)
        y_lbl = Text("σ(z)", font_size=20, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.get_y_axis(), UP, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(axes), FadeIn(x_lbl), FadeIn(y_lbl))

        sigmoid_curve = axes.plot(lambda z: 1 / (1 + np.exp(-z)), x_range=[-6, 6], color=BLUE_C, stroke_width=3)
        self.play(Create(sigmoid_curve))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Mark asymptotes at 0 and 1
        dash0 = DashedLine(axes.c2p(-6, 0), axes.c2p(6, 0), color=GREY_C, stroke_width=1.5)
        dash1 = DashedLine(axes.c2p(-6, 1), axes.c2p(6, 1), color=GREY_C, stroke_width=1.5)
        lbl0 = Text("0", font_size=18, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.c2p(-6, 0), LEFT, buff=0.1)
        lbl1 = Text("1", font_size=18, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.c2p(-6, 1), LEFT, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(dash0), Create(dash1), FadeIn(lbl0), FadeIn(lbl1))

        # Highlight saturation zones
        sat_left = axes.get_area(sigmoid_curve, x_range=[-6, -3.5], color=RED, opacity=0.25)
        sat_right = axes.get_area(sigmoid_curve, x_range=[3.5, 6], color=RED, opacity=0.25)
        self.play(FadeIn(sat_left), FadeIn(sat_right))
        sat_note = Text("Saturation zones: σ'(z) ≈ 0 → vanishing gradients", font_size=18, color=RED)
        sat_note.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(FadeIn(sat_note))
        self.wait(1.5)

        # Show the derivative peak at z=0
        peak_dot = Dot(axes.c2p(0, 0.5), radius=0.12, color=YELLOW)
        peak_lbl = Text("σ'(0) = 0.25 (max)", font_size=18, color=YELLOW)
        peak_lbl.next_to(peak_dot, RIGHT, buff=0.2)
        self.play(Create(peak_dot), FadeIn(peak_lbl))
        self.wait(2.0)

description: Demonstrates matrix-vector multiplication as a geometric transformation by animating how a 2x2 matrix shears and scales basis vectors and an example vector in 2D space.
code:
class LinearTransformationScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        import numpy as np

        title = Text("Matrix as a Linear Transformation", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-3.5, 3.5, 1],
            y_range=[-2.5, 3.5, 1],
            x_length=7.0,
            y_length=5.5,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_D},
        ).move_to(ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.3)
        self.play(Create(axes))

        # Original basis vectors
        e1 = Arrow(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(1,0), buff=0, color=GREEN, stroke_width=4)
        e2 = Arrow(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(0,1), buff=0, color=RED, stroke_width=4)
        lbl_e1 = Text("e₁", font_size=20, color=GREEN).next_to(axes.c2p(1,0), DOWN, buff=0.1)
        lbl_e2 = Text("e₂", font_size=20, color=RED).next_to(axes.c2p(0,1), LEFT, buff=0.1)
        v = Arrow(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(1,2), buff=0, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=4)
        lbl_v = Text("v", font_size=20, color=YELLOW).next_to(axes.c2p(1,2), RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(e1), Create(e2), FadeIn(lbl_e1), FadeIn(lbl_e2))
        self.play(Create(v), FadeIn(lbl_v))
        self.wait(0.6)

        # Show the matrix
        mat_txt = Text("A = [[2, 1], [0, 1]]", font_size=22, color=YELLOW)
        mat_txt.to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(mat_txt))
        self.wait(0.8)

        # Transform: e1 -> (2,0), e2 -> (1,1), v=(1,2) -> (4,2)
        self.play(
            e1.animate.put_start_and_end_on(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(2,0)),
            e2.animate.put_start_and_end_on(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(1,1)),
            v.animate.put_start_and_end_on(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(4,2)),
            lbl_e1.animate.move_to(axes.c2p(2.2, -0.3)),
            lbl_e2.animate.move_to(axes.c2p(0.8, 1.2)),
            lbl_v.animate.move_to(axes.c2p(4.2, 2.1)),
            run_time=2.0
        )
        self.wait(0.5)
        result = Text("Av = [2·1+1·2, 0·1+1·2] = [4, 2]", font_size=20, color=GREEN)
        result.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(FadeIn(result))
        self.wait(2.0)

description: Visualizes the concept of eigenvectors and eigenvalues by showing how a 2x2 matrix stretches its eigenvectors along their own directions while rotating other vectors.
code:
class EigenvectorScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        import numpy as np

        title = Text("Eigenvectors and Eigenvalues", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-3.5, 3.5, 1],
            y_range=[-3.0, 3.5, 1],
            x_length=7.0,
            y_length=5.5,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_D},
        ).move_to(ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.3)
        self.play(Create(axes))

        # Eigenvector 1: direction (1,0), eigenvalue 3
        ev1_before = Arrow(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(1,0), buff=0, color=GREEN, stroke_width=4)
        lbl1 = Text("λ₁=3, v₁=[1,0]", font_size=20, color=GREEN).next_to(axes.c2p(1,0), UP, buff=0.15)
        # Eigenvector 2: direction (0,1), eigenvalue 1
        ev2_before = Arrow(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(0,1), buff=0, color=RED, stroke_width=4)
        lbl2 = Text("λ₂=1, v₂=[0,1]", font_size=20, color=RED).next_to(axes.c2p(0,1), RIGHT, buff=0.15)
        # A random non-eigenvector
        rand_v = Arrow(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(1,1), buff=0, color=GREY_B, stroke_width=3)
        rand_lbl = Text("w=[1,1] (not an eigenvec)", font_size=18, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.c2p(1,1), RIGHT, buff=0.1)

        self.play(Create(ev1_before), FadeIn(lbl1))
        self.play(Create(ev2_before), FadeIn(lbl2))
        self.play(Create(rand_v), FadeIn(rand_lbl))
        self.wait(0.8)

        mat_lbl = Text("A = diag(3, 1)", font_size=22, color=YELLOW)
        mat_lbl.to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.4)
        self.play(FadeIn(mat_lbl))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Apply transform: e1 scaled by 3, e2 unchanged, w -> (3,1)
        self.play(
            ev1_before.animate.put_start_and_end_on(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(3,0)),
            lbl1.animate.move_to(axes.c2p(3.2, 0.35)),
            rand_v.animate.put_start_and_end_on(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(3,1)),
            rand_lbl.animate.move_to(axes.c2p(3.5, 1.1)),
            run_time=2.0
        )
        self.wait(0.5)
        note = Text("λ₁=3: e₁ stretches 3× | λ₂=1: e₂ unchanged | w rotates", font_size=18, color=YELLOW)
        note.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(FadeIn(note))
        self.wait(2.0)

description: Explains dot product and vector projection by animating how one vector's shadow falls onto another, showing the geometric meaning of cos(θ) and orthogonality.
code:
class DotProductProjectionScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        import numpy as np

        title = Text("Dot Product and Vector Projection", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-0.5, 4.5, 1],
            y_range=[-0.5, 3.5, 1],
            x_length=7.0,
            y_length=4.5,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_D},
        ).move_to(ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.5)
        self.play(Create(axes))

        # Vectors a and b
        a = np.array([4, 0])
        b = np.array([3, 2])
        va = Arrow(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(*a), buff=0, color=GREEN, stroke_width=4)
        vb = Arrow(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(*b), buff=0, color=BLUE_C, stroke_width=4)
        la = Text("a", font_size=22, color=GREEN).next_to(axes.c2p(*a), RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        lb = Text("b", font_size=22, color=BLUE_C).next_to(axes.c2p(*b), UP, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(va), Create(vb), FadeIn(la), FadeIn(lb))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Projection of b onto a
        dot = float(np.dot(a, b))
        a_norm_sq = float(np.dot(a, a))
        proj_scalar = dot / max(a_norm_sq, 0.001)
        proj_pt = np.array([proj_scalar * a[0], proj_scalar * a[1]])
        proj_arrow = Arrow(axes.c2p(0,0), axes.c2p(*proj_pt), buff=0, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=4)

class DerivativeAsLimitScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        import numpy as np

        title = Text("Derivative as a Limit of Secant Lines", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-0.5, 3.5, 1],
            y_range=[-0.5, 10.0, 2],
            x_length=7.5,
            y_length=4.8,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_B},
        ).move_to(ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.35)
        x_lbl = Text("x", font_size=18, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.get_x_axis(), RIGHT, buff=0.15)
        y_lbl = Text("f(x)=x²", font_size=18, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.get_y_axis(), UP, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(axes), FadeIn(x_lbl), FadeIn(y_lbl))

        curve = axes.plot(lambda x: x**2, x_range=[0, 3.2], color=BLUE_C, stroke_width=3)
        self.play(Create(curve))

        x0 = 1.0
        p0 = axes.c2p(x0, x0**2)
        dot0 = Dot(p0, radius=0.1, color=GREEN)
        lbl_x0 = Text("x₀", font_size=18, color=GREEN).next_to(p0, LEFT, buff=0.1)
        self.play(FadeIn(dot0), FadeIn(lbl_x0))

        h_values = [1.5, 1.0, 0.6, 0.3, 0.1, 0.02]
        secant_line = None
        h_lbl = None

        for h in h_values:
            x1 = x0 + h
            slope = (x1**2 - x0**2) / max(h, 1e-6)
            # Secant line: y - y0 = slope*(x - x0)
            x_left = max(x0 - 0.8, 0.0)
            x_right = min(x1 + 0.5, 3.3)
            new_secant = axes.plot(lambda x, s=slope, xx=x0: x0**2 + s*(x - xx),
                                   x_range=[x_left, x_right], color=YELLOW, stroke_width=2.5)
            dot1 = Dot(axes.c2p(x1, x1**2), radius=0.08, color=RED)
            new_h_lbl = Text(f"h = {h:.2f}  →  slope ≈ {slope:.2f}", font_size=18, color=YELLOW)
            new_h_lbl.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
            if secant_line is None:
                self.play(Create(new_secant), FadeIn(dot1), FadeIn(new_h_lbl))
            else:
                self.play(Transform(secant_line, new_secant), FadeOut(h_lbl), FadeIn(new_h_lbl), FadeOut(dot1))
            secant_line = new_secant
            h_lbl = new_h_lbl
            self.wait(0.6)

        tangent = axes.plot(lambda x: x0**2 + 2*x0*(x - x0), x_range=[0.2, 2.0], color=GREEN, stroke_width=3)
        self.play(Transform(secant_line, tangent), FadeOut(h_lbl))
        final = Text("f'(x₀) = lim h→0 [f(x₀+h)-f(x₀)]/h = 2x₀", font_size=19, color=GREEN)
        final.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(FadeIn(final))
        self.wait(2.0)

class RiemannSumScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        import numpy as np

        title = Text("Riemann Sum → Definite Integral", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-0.2, 3.6, 1],
            y_range=[-0.2, 5.5, 1],
            x_length=7.5,
            y_length=4.5,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_B},
        ).move_to(ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.4)
        x_lbl = Text("x", font_size=18, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.get_x_axis(), RIGHT, buff=0.15)
        y_lbl = Text("f(x)", font_size=18, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.get_y_axis(), UP, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(axes), FadeIn(x_lbl), FadeIn(y_lbl))

        f = lambda x: 0.5 * x**2 + 0.5
        curve = axes.plot(f, x_range=[0, 3.5], color=BLUE_C, stroke_width=3)
        self.play(Create(curve))
        self.wait(0.4)

        a, b = 0.5, 3.0
        rects_group = None
        n_label = None

        for n in [4, 8, 16, 32]:
            dx = (b - a) / n
            xs = [a + i * dx for i in range(n)]
            rects = VGroup(*[
                Rectangle(
                    width=abs(axes.c2p(x + dx, 0)[0] - axes.c2p(x, 0)[0]) - 0.02,
                    height=abs(axes.c2p(0, f(x + dx/2))[1] - axes.c2p(0, 0)[1]),
                    fill_color=TEAL,
                    fill_opacity=0.45,
                    stroke_width=0.8,
                    color=TEAL
                ).move_to([
                    (axes.c2p(x, 0)[0] + axes.c2p(x + dx, 0)[0]) / 2,
                    (axes.c2p(0, f(x + dx/2))[1] + axes.c2p(0, 0)[1]) / 2,
                    0
                ])
                for x in xs
            ])
            approx = sum(f(x + dx/2) * dx for x in xs)
            new_n_lbl = Text(f"n={n} rectangles  ≈  {approx:.3f}", font_size=19, color=YELLOW)
            new_n_lbl.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
            if rects_group is None:
                self.play(FadeIn(rects), FadeIn(new_n_lbl))
            else:
                self.play(Transform(rects_group, rects), Transform(n_label, new_n_lbl))
            rects_group = rects
            n_label = new_n_lbl
            self.wait(0.8)

        exact = 0.5 * (b**3 - a**3) / 3 + 0.5 * (b - a)
        final = Text(f"∫ f(x)dx = {exact:.3f}  (exact)", font_size=20, color=GREEN)
        final.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(Transform(n_label, final))
        self.wait(2.0)

class ChainRuleScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("The Chain Rule", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        center = ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.1

        # Step 1: composite function
        step1_lbl = Text("Composite function: h(x) = f(g(x))", font_size=24, color=YELLOW)
        step1_lbl.move_to(center + UP * 1.6)
        self.play(FadeIn(step1_lbl))

        # Show boxes: x -> g -> f -> h
        box_x = RoundedRectangle(width=1.4, height=0.8, color=GREY_B, fill_opacity=0.2).move_to(center + LEFT * 4)
        box_g = RoundedRectangle(width=1.4, height=0.8, color=BLUE_C, fill_opacity=0.2).move_to(center + LEFT * 1.5)
        box_f = RoundedRectangle(width=1.4, height=0.8, color=GREEN, fill_opacity=0.2).move_to(center + RIGHT * 1.5)
        box_h = RoundedRectangle(width=1.4, height=0.8, color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.2).move_to(center + RIGHT * 4)
        lx = Text("x", font_size=22).move_to(box_x)
        lg = Text("g(x)", font_size=22).move_to(box_g)
        lf = Text("f(u)", font_size=22).move_to(box_f)
        lh = Text("h(x)", font_size=22).move_to(box_h)

        a1 = Arrow(box_x.get_right(), box_g.get_left(), buff=0.1, color=WHITE, stroke_width=2)
        a2 = Arrow(box_g.get_right(), box_f.get_left(), buff=0.1, color=WHITE, stroke_width=2)
        a3 = Arrow(box_f.get_right(), box_h.get_left(), buff=0.1, color=WHITE, stroke_width=2)
        u_lbl = Text("u=g(x)", font_size=14, color=GREY_B).next_to(a2, UP, buff=0.1)

        self.play(LaggedStart(Create(box_x), Create(box_g), Create(box_f), Create(box_h), lag_ratio=0.15))
        self.play(LaggedStart(FadeIn(lx), FadeIn(lg), FadeIn(lf), FadeIn(lh), lag_ratio=0.1))
        self.play(Create(a1), Create(a2), Create(a3), FadeIn(u_lbl))
        self.wait(0.8)

        # Step 2: chain rule formula
        self.play(FadeOut(step1_lbl))
        step2_lbl = Text("Chain rule: dh/dx = df/du · dg/dx", font_size=24, color=YELLOW)
        step2_lbl.move_to(center + UP * 1.6)
        self.play(FadeIn(step2_lbl))

        # Highlight df/du and dg/dx separately
        self.play(Circumscribe(box_f, color=GREEN, run_time=0.8))
        df_lbl = Text("df/du", font_size=18, color=GREEN).next_to(box_f, DOWN, buff=0.25)
        self.play(FadeIn(df_lbl))
        self.play(Circumscribe(box_g, color=BLUE_C, run_time=0.8))
        dg_lbl = Text("dg/dx", font_size=18, color=BLUE_C).next_to(box_g, DOWN, buff=0.25)
        self.play(FadeIn(dg_lbl))
        self.wait(0.8)

        # Step 3: concrete example
        self.play(FadeOut(VGroup(step2_lbl, df_lbl, dg_lbl)))
        example = Text("Example: h(x) = sin(x²)", font_size=22, color=WHITE)
        example.move_to(center + UP * 1.6)
        deriv = Text("h'(x) = cos(x²) · 2x", font_size=24, color=GREEN)
        deriv.move_to(center + DOWN * 1.6)
        self.play(FadeIn(example))
        self.play(Write(deriv))
        self.wait(2.0)

class SimpleHarmonicMotionScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        import numpy as np

        title = Text("Simple Harmonic Motion", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        # Spring-mass on the left side
        wall = Rectangle(width=0.25, height=2.0, color=GREY_B, fill_opacity=0.8)
        wall.move_to(LEFT * 5.5 + DOWN * 0.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(wall))

        # Plot axes on the right
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 6.3, 1],
            y_range=[-1.5, 1.5, 1],
            x_length=5.5,
            y_length=2.8,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_B},
        ).move_to(RIGHT * 1.5 + DOWN * 0.5)
        t_lbl = Text("t", font_size=18, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.get_x_axis(), RIGHT, buff=0.15)
        x_lbl = Text("x(t)", font_size=18, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.get_y_axis(), UP, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(axes), FadeIn(t_lbl), FadeIn(x_lbl))

        A = 1.0
        omega = 1.0
        eq_x = LEFT * 4.0 + DOWN * 0.5

        mass = Square(side_length=0.5, color=ORANGE, fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.8)
        mass.move_to(eq_x)
        spring = Line(wall.get_right(), mass.get_left(), color=GREY_B, stroke_width=2)
        mass_lbl = Text("m", font_size=18).move_to(mass)

        self.play(Create(mass), FadeIn(mass_lbl))
        spring = always_redraw(lambda: Line(wall.get_right(), mass.get_left(), color=GREY_B, stroke_width=2))
        self.add(spring)

        t_tracker = ValueTracker(0)
        curve = always_redraw(lambda: axes.plot(
            lambda t: A * np.cos(omega * t),
            x_range=[0, min(t_tracker.get_value(), 6.28)],
            color=BLUE_C, stroke_width=3
        ))
        self.add(curve)

        def update_mass(mob):
            t = t_tracker.get_value()
            x_disp = A * np.cos(omega * t)
            mob.move_to(eq_x + RIGHT * x_disp * 1.5)

        mass.add_updater(update_mass)
        mass_lbl.add_updater(lambda m: m.move_to(mass.get_center()))

        self.play(t_tracker.animate.set_value(6.28), run_time=4.0, rate_func=linear)
        mass.remove_updater(update_mass)
        mass_lbl.clear_updaters()

        formula = Text("x(t) = A cos(ωt)  |  ω² = k/m", font_size=20, color=GREEN)
        formula.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(FadeIn(formula))
        self.wait(2.0)

class WaveSuperpositionScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        import numpy as np

        title = Text("Wave Superposition & Interference", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        def make_axes(shift):
            return Axes(
                x_range=[0, 4*np.pi, np.pi],
                y_range=[-2.5, 2.5, 1],
                x_length=7.0,
                y_length=1.6,
                axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_D},
            ).move_to(shift)

        ax1 = make_axes(UP * 1.55 + DOWN * 0.1)
        ax2 = make_axes(ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.1)
        ax3 = make_axes(DOWN * 1.55 + DOWN * 0.1)

        lbl1 = Text("Wave 1: sin(x)", font_size=16, color=BLUE_C).next_to(ax1, LEFT, buff=0.15)
        lbl2 = Text("Wave 2: sin(x+π/3)", font_size=16, color=GREEN).next_to(ax2, LEFT, buff=0.15)
        lbl3 = Text("Sum", font_size=16, color=ORANGE).next_to(ax3, LEFT, buff=0.15)

        self.play(Create(ax1), Create(ax2), Create(ax3))
        self.play(FadeIn(lbl1), FadeIn(lbl2), FadeIn(lbl3))

        w1 = ax1.plot(lambda x: np.sin(x), x_range=[0, 4*np.pi], color=BLUE_C, stroke_width=2.5)
        w2 = ax2.plot(lambda x: np.sin(x + np.pi/3), x_range=[0, 4*np.pi], color=GREEN, stroke_width=2.5)
        w_sum = ax3.plot(lambda x: np.sin(x) + np.sin(x + np.pi/3), x_range=[0, 4*np.pi], color=ORANGE, stroke_width=2.5)
        self.play(Create(w1), Create(w2))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(Create(w_sum))
        self.wait(0.8)

        # Show destructive interference case
        note = Text("Phase shift π/3 → partial constructive interference", font_size=18, color=YELLOW)
        note.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.35)
        self.play(FadeIn(note))
        self.wait(1.0)

        # Replace wave 2 with antiphase (destructive)
        self.play(FadeOut(note))
        w2_dest = ax2.plot(lambda x: np.sin(x + np.pi), x_range=[0, 4*np.pi], color=RED, stroke_width=2.5)
        w_sum_dest = ax3.plot(lambda x: np.sin(x) + np.sin(x + np.pi), x_range=[0, 4*np.pi], color=GREY_B, stroke_width=2.5)
        lbl2_dest = Text("Wave 2: sin(x+π)", font_size=16, color=RED).next_to(ax2, LEFT, buff=0.15)
        self.play(Transform(w2, w2_dest), Transform(lbl2, lbl2_dest))
        self.play(Transform(w_sum, w_sum_dest))
        dest_note = Text("Phase shift π → complete destructive interference (sum = 0)", font_size=17, color=RED)
        dest_note.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.35)
        self.play(FadeIn(dest_note))
        self.wait(2.5)

class FourierTransformScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        import numpy as np

        title = Text("Discrete Fourier Transform", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        # Synthesize a signal: 2*sin(2t) + sin(5t)
        N = 256
        t_vals = np.linspace(0, 2*np.pi, N)
        signal = 2*np.sin(2*t_vals) + np.sin(5*t_vals)

        # Time-domain axes
        ax_t = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 6.3, np.pi],
            y_range=[-3.5, 3.5, 1],
            x_length=7.0,
            y_length=2.2,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_B},
        ).move_to(UP * 1.5)
        t_lbl = Text("time", font_size=16, color=GREY_B).next_to(ax_t.get_x_axis(), RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        s_lbl = Text("x(t)", font_size=16, color=GREY_B).next_to(ax_t.get_y_axis(), UP, buff=0.08)
        sig_plot = ax_t.plot(lambda t: 2*np.sin(2*t) + np.sin(5*t), x_range=[0, 2*np.pi], color=BLUE_C, stroke_width=2.5)
        self.play(Create(ax_t), FadeIn(t_lbl), FadeIn(s_lbl))
        self.play(Create(sig_plot))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Frequency-domain (DFT magnitude)
        freqs = np.fft.rfftfreq(N, d=1/N)
        magnitudes = np.abs(np.fft.rfft(signal)) / N * 2
        ax_f = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 10, 2],
            y_range=[0, 2.5, 1],
            x_length=7.0,
            y_length=2.2,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_B},
        ).move_to(DOWN * 1.5)
        f_lbl = Text("frequency", font_size=16, color=GREY_B).next_to(ax_f.get_x_axis(), RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        a_lbl = Text("|X(f)|", font_size=16, color=GREY_B).next_to(ax_f.get_y_axis(), UP, buff=0.08)
        self.play(Create(ax_f), FadeIn(f_lbl), FadeIn(a_lbl))

        # Draw bars for the dominant frequencies
        prominent = [(2, 2.0), (5, 1.0)]
        bars = VGroup()
        for freq, amp in prominent:
            bar = ax_f.get_vertical_line(ax_f.c2p(freq, amp), line_config={"stroke_width": 6, "color": ORANGE})
            bars.add(bar)
        self.play(Create(bars))

        peak2 = Text("f=2, A=2", font_size=16, color=ORANGE).next_to(ax_f.c2p(2, 2.0), UR, buff=0.1)
        peak5 = Text("f=5, A=1", font_size=16, color=ORANGE).next_to(ax_f.c2p(5, 1.0), UR, buff=0.1)
        self.play(FadeIn(peak2), FadeIn(peak5))
        formula = Text("x(t) = 2·sin(2t) + sin(5t)", font_size=19, color=YELLOW)
        formula.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.35)
        self.play(FadeIn(formula))
        self.wait(2.5)

        import numpy as np

        title = Text("1D Convolution: Sliding Kernel", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        # Signal values
        signal = [1, 2, 3, 2, 1, 0, 1, 2]
        kernel = [0.25, 0.5, 0.25]  # smoothing kernel
        n_s = len(signal)
        n_k = len(kernel)

        cell_w = 0.72
        sig_start = LEFT * 3.2 + UP * 0.9

        # Draw signal cells
        sig_cells = VGroup()
        sig_lbls = VGroup()
        for i, v in enumerate(signal):
            cell = Square(side_length=cell_w, color=BLUE_C, fill_color=BLUE_C, fill_opacity=0.2)
            cell.move_to(sig_start + RIGHT * i * cell_w)
            lbl = Text(str(v), font_size=18).move_to(cell)
            sig_cells.add(cell)
            sig_lbls.add(lbl)
        sig_title = Text("Signal x", font_size=18, color=BLUE_C).next_to(sig_cells, LEFT, buff=0.2)
        self.play(Create(sig_cells), FadeIn(sig_lbls), FadeIn(sig_title))
        self.wait(0.3)

        # Draw kernel cells
        kern_start = LEFT * 1.1 + DOWN * 0.3
        kern_cells = VGroup()
        kern_lbls = VGroup()
        for i, v in enumerate(kernel):
            cell = Square(side_length=cell_w, color=ORANGE, fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.35)
            cell.move_to(kern_start + RIGHT * i * cell_w)
            lbl = Text(str(v), font_size=16).move_to(cell)
            kern_cells.add(cell)
            kern_lbls.add(lbl)
        kern_title = Text("Kernel h", font_size=18, color=ORANGE).next_to(kern_cells, LEFT, buff=0.2)
        self.play(Create(kern_cells), FadeIn(kern_lbls), FadeIn(kern_title))
        self.wait(0.3)

        # Draw output cells
        out_start = LEFT * 2.5 + DOWN * 1.6
        out_cells = VGroup()
        out_lbls = VGroup()
        output = np.convolve(signal, kernel, mode='valid')
        for i in range(len(output)):
            cell = Square(side_length=cell_w, color=GREEN, fill_color=GREEN, fill_opacity=0.2)
            cell.move_to(out_start + RIGHT * i * cell_w)
            out_cells.add(cell)
        out_title = Text("Output y", font_size=18, color=GREEN).next_to(out_cells, LEFT, buff=0.2)
        self.play(Create(out_cells), FadeIn(out_title))

        # Slide kernel and fill output
        for i in range(len(output)):
            shift = RIGHT * i * cell_w
            target_pos = sig_start + RIGHT * i * cell_w + RIGHT * cell_w
            self.play(
                kern_cells.animate.move_to(target_pos + RIGHT * (n_k-1)/2 * cell_w + DOWN * 1.2),
                kern_lbls.animate.move_to(target_pos + RIGHT * (n_k-1)/2 * cell_w + DOWN * 1.2),
                run_time=0.4
            )
            result_lbl = Text(f"{output[i]:.2f}", font_size=16, color=GREEN)
            result_lbl.move_to(out_cells[i])
            out_lbls.add(result_lbl)
            self.play(FadeIn(result_lbl), run_time=0.3)

        self.wait(2.0)

class NyquistSamplingScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        import numpy as np

        title = Text("Nyquist Sampling Theorem", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        f_signal = 3.0  # Hz

        def make_axes(shift):
            return Axes(
                x_range=[0, 2.0, 0.5],
                y_range=[-1.5, 1.5, 1],
                x_length=7.5,
                y_length=1.9,
                axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_D},
            ).move_to(shift)

        ax1 = make_axes(UP * 1.5)
        ax2 = make_axes(DOWN * 0.3)
        ax3 = make_axes(DOWN * 2.1)

        lbl1 = Text("Original signal f=3Hz", font_size=16, color=BLUE_C).next_to(ax1, RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        lbl2 = Text("Sampled at fₛ=8Hz (> 2f) ✓", font_size=16, color=GREEN).next_to(ax2, RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        lbl3 = Text("Sampled at fₛ=4Hz (= 2f) ✓", font_size=16, color=YELLOW).next_to(ax3, RIGHT, buff=0.1)

        sig = ax1.plot(lambda t: np.sin(2*np.pi*f_signal*t), x_range=[0, 2.0], color=BLUE_C, stroke_width=2.5)
        self.play(Create(ax1), FadeIn(lbl1), Create(sig))
        self.wait(0.4)

        # Adequate sampling (fs=8)
        self.play(Create(ax2), FadeIn(lbl2))
        ts_good = np.arange(0, 2.0, 1/8.0)
        good_dots = VGroup(*[Dot(ax2.c2p(t, np.sin(2*np.pi*f_signal*t)), radius=0.07, color=GREEN) for t in ts_good])
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(d) for d in good_dots], lag_ratio=0.05))
        reconstructed = ax2.plot(lambda t: np.sin(2*np.pi*f_signal*t), x_range=[0, 2.0], color=GREEN, stroke_width=2)
        self.play(Create(reconstructed))
        self.wait(0.6)

        # Nyquist-rate sampling (fs=4)
        self.play(Create(ax3), FadeIn(lbl3))
        ts_nyq = np.arange(0, 2.0, 1/4.0)
        nyq_dots = VGroup(*[Dot(ax3.c2p(t, np.sin(2*np.pi*f_signal*t)), radius=0.07, color=YELLOW) for t in ts_nyq])
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(d) for d in nyq_dots], lag_ratio=0.08))
        nyq_recon = ax3.plot(lambda t: np.sin(2*np.pi*f_signal*t), x_range=[0, 2.0], color=YELLOW, stroke_width=2)
        self.play(Create(nyq_recon))

        rule = Text("Nyquist rate: fₛ ≥ 2f_max (Shannon-Nyquist theorem)", font_size=18, color=WHITE)
        rule.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.3)
        self.play(FadeIn(rule))
        self.wait(2.5)

class DijkstraScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Dijkstra's Shortest Path Algorithm", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        center = ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.3

        # Node positions
        positions = {
            "A": center + LEFT * 3.5,
            "B": center + LEFT * 1.0 + UP * 1.5,
            "C": center + LEFT * 1.0 + DOWN * 1.5,
            "D": center + RIGHT * 1.5 + UP * 0.8,
            "E": center + RIGHT * 3.5,
        }
        # Edges (u, v, weight)
        edges = [("A","B",4),("A","C",2),("B","D",3),("C","D",5),("C","E",8),("D","E",1)]

        # Draw edges
        edge_objs = {}
        for u, v, w in edges:
            line = Line(positions[u], positions[v], color=GREY_B, stroke_width=2)
            w_lbl = Text(str(w), font_size=16, color=GREY_B)
            mid = (positions[u] + positions[v]) / 2
            w_lbl.move_to(mid + UP * 0.2)
            self.play(Create(line), FadeIn(w_lbl), run_time=0.2)
            edge_objs[(u,v)] = line

        # Draw nodes
        node_objs = {}
        node_lbls = {}
        for name, pos in positions.items():
            circle = Circle(radius=0.32, color=BLUE_C, fill_color=BLUE_C, fill_opacity=0.25).move_to(pos)
            lbl = Text(name, font_size=20).move_to(pos)
            self.play(Create(circle), FadeIn(lbl), run_time=0.2)
            node_objs[name] = circle
            node_lbls[name] = lbl

        self.wait(0.5)

        # Dijkstra steps: dist from A
        steps = [
            ("A", {"A":0, "B":"∞", "C":"∞", "D":"∞", "E":"∞"}, None),
            ("A", {"A":0, "B":"4", "C":"2", "D":"∞", "E":"∞"}, "A"),
            ("C", {"A":0, "B":"4", "C":"2", "D":"7", "E":"10"}, "C"),
            ("B", {"A":0, "B":"4", "C":"2", "D":"7", "E":"10"}, "B"),
            ("D", {"A":0, "B":"4", "C":"2", "D":"7", "E":"8"}, "D"),
        ]

        dist_txt = None
        for visit, dists, visited_node in steps:
            dist_str = "  ".join(f"{k}:{v}" for k,v in dists.items())
            new_txt = Text(f"dist: {dist_str}", font_size=16, color=YELLOW)
            new_txt.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
            if visited_node:
                self.play(node_objs[visited_node].animate.set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=0.7), run_time=0.4)
            if dist_txt is None:
                self.play(FadeIn(new_txt))
            else:
                self.play(Transform(dist_txt, new_txt))
            dist_txt = new_txt
            self.wait(0.7)

        # Highlight shortest path A->C->D->E
        path = [("A","C"),("C","D"),("D","E")]
        for u, v in path:
            line = edge_objs.get((u,v)) or edge_objs.get((v,u))
            if line:
                self.play(line.animate.set_color(GREEN).set_stroke(width=5), run_time=0.4)
        final = Text("Shortest path A→E: A→C→D→E, cost=8", font_size=18, color=GREEN)
        final.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(Transform(dist_txt, final))
        self.wait(2.5)

class KruskalMSTScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Kruskal's Minimum Spanning Tree", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        center = ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.3

        positions = {
            "A": center + LEFT * 3.2 + UP * 1.2,
            "B": center + RIGHT * 0.5 + UP * 1.8,
            "C": center + RIGHT * 3.2 + UP * 0.5,
            "D": center + LEFT * 0.5 + DOWN * 1.2,
            "E": center + RIGHT * 2.0 + DOWN * 1.5,
        }
        # (u, v, weight) sorted by weight for Kruskal
        edges_sorted = [
            ("A","D",1), ("D","E",2), ("A","B",3), ("B","D",4),
            ("B","C",5), ("C","E",6), ("A","C",7)
        ]

        # Draw all edges first (grey)
        all_edge_objs = {}
        for u, v, w in edges_sorted:
            line = Line(positions[u], positions[v], color=GREY_C, stroke_width=1.5)
            mid = (positions[u] + positions[v]) / 2
            w_lbl = Text(str(w), font_size=14, color=GREY_B).move_to(mid + UP * 0.18)
            self.play(Create(line), FadeIn(w_lbl), run_time=0.15)
            all_edge_objs[(u,v)] = line

        # Draw nodes
        for name, pos in positions.items():
            c = Circle(radius=0.30, color=BLUE_C, fill_color=BLUE_C, fill_opacity=0.25).move_to(pos)
            lbl = Text(name, font_size=20).move_to(pos)
            self.play(Create(c), FadeIn(lbl), run_time=0.15)

        self.wait(0.5)

        # Kruskal: add edges greedily (precomputed: A-D, D-E, A-B, B-C accepted; others skipped)
        mst_edges = [("A","D",1),("D","E",2),("A","B",3),("B","C",5)]
        skip_edges = [("B","D",4)]  # would create cycle
        status_lbl = None

        for u, v, w in mst_edges:
            line = all_edge_objs.get((u,v)) or all_edge_objs.get((v,u))
            new_lbl = Text(f"Add edge {u}-{v} (weight {w}) ✓", font_size=18, color=GREEN)
            new_lbl.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
            if line:
                self.play(line.animate.set_color(GREEN).set_stroke(width=5), run_time=0.5)
            if status_lbl:
                self.play(Transform(status_lbl, new_lbl))
            else:
                self.play(FadeIn(new_lbl))
            status_lbl = new_lbl
            self.wait(0.6)

        for u, v, w in skip_edges:
            line = all_edge_objs.get((u,v)) or all_edge_objs.get((v,u))
            skip_lbl = Text(f"Skip edge {u}-{v} (weight {w}) ✗ cycle", font_size=18, color=RED)
            skip_lbl.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
            self.play(Transform(status_lbl, skip_lbl))
            status_lbl = skip_lbl
            self.wait(0.6)

        final = Text("MST total weight: 1+2+3+5 = 11", font_size=18, color=GREEN)
        final.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(Transform(status_lbl, final))
        self.wait(2.5)

class CentralLimitTheoremScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        import numpy as np

        title = Text("Central Limit Theorem", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        np.random.seed(42)
        n_samples = 5000
        sample_sizes = [1, 5, 30]
        axes_shifts = [LEFT * 4.0, ORIGIN, RIGHT * 4.0]
        colors = [BLUE_C, ORANGE, GREEN]

        for n, shift, col in zip(sample_sizes, axes_shifts, colors):
            means = np.mean(np.random.uniform(0, 1, size=(n_samples, n)), axis=1)
            counts, bin_edges = np.histogram(means, bins=20, density=True)
            max_count = max(counts)

            ax = Axes(
                x_range=[0, 1, 0.5],
                y_range=[0, max_count * 1.15, max_count * 0.5],
                x_length=2.8,
                y_length=3.0,
                axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_D},
            ).move_to(shift + DOWN * 0.3)
            n_lbl = Text(f"n={n}", font_size=18, color=col).next_to(ax, UP, buff=0.15)
            self.play(Create(ax), FadeIn(n_lbl), run_time=0.4)

            rects = VGroup()
            for i, c in enumerate(counts):
                bw = bin_edges[i+1] - bin_edges[i]
                rect = Rectangle(
                    width=abs(ax.c2p(bw, 0)[0] - ax.c2p(0, 0)[0]) - 0.01,
                    height=abs(ax.c2p(0, c)[1] - ax.c2p(0, 0)[1]),
                    fill_color=col, fill_opacity=0.55, stroke_width=0.5, color=col
                ).move_to([
                    (ax.c2p(bin_edges[i], 0)[0] + ax.c2p(bin_edges[i+1], 0)[0]) / 2,
                    (ax.c2p(0, c)[1] + ax.c2p(0, 0)[1]) / 2,
                    0
                ])
                rects.add(rect)
            self.play(FadeIn(rects), run_time=0.6)

        note = Text("As n→∞, distribution of sample means → Normal(0.5, σ²/n)", font_size=18, color=YELLOW)
        note.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(FadeIn(note))
        self.wait(2.5)

class BayesTheoremScene(Scene):

        # Given values

            ("P(Disease)", "0.01", "1% prevalence (prior)", BLUE_C),

        self.play(Create(result_box), FadeIn(result))

        self.wait(2.5)

description: Demonstrates the law of large numbers by animating a running average of coin flip outcomes converging to the true probability of 0.5 as the number of trials grows.
code:
class LawOfLargeNumbersScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        import numpy as np

        title = Text("Law of Large Numbers", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.4)

        np.random.seed(7)
        n_flips = 300
        flips = np.random.randint(0, 2, n_flips)  # 0=tails, 1=heads
        running_avg = np.cumsum(flips) / np.arange(1, n_flips + 1)

        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, n_flips, 50],
            y_range=[0, 1.05, 0.25],
            x_length=8.0,
            y_length=4.5,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "color": GREY_B},
        ).move_to(ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.4)
        x_lbl = Text("Number of flips (n)", font_size=17, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.get_x_axis(), RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        y_lbl = Text("P(Heads)", font_size=17, color=GREY_B).next_to(axes.get_y_axis(), UP, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(axes), FadeIn(x_lbl), FadeIn(y_lbl))

        # True probability line
        true_line = DashedLine(axes.c2p(0, 0.5), axes.c2p(n_flips, 0.5), color=YELLOW, stroke_width=2)
        true_lbl = Text("p = 0.5", font_size=16, color=YELLOW).next_to(axes.c2p(n_flips, 0.5), RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(true_line), FadeIn(true_lbl))

        # Animate running average as a path
        path_points = [axes.c2p(i + 1, running_avg[i]) for i in range(n_flips)]
        running_path = VMobject(color=BLUE_C, stroke_width=2.5)
        running_path.set_points_as_corners(path_points[:2])
        self.add(running_path)

        n_tracker = ValueTracker(2)

        def update_path(mob):
            n = int(n_tracker.get_value())
            pts = [axes.c2p(i + 1, running_avg[i]) for i in range(max(2, n))]
            mob.set_points_as_corners(pts)

        running_path.add_updater(update_path)

        counter = always_redraw(lambda: Text(
            f"n={int(n_tracker.get_value())}  avg={running_avg[int(n_tracker.get_value())-1]:.3f}",
            font_size=18, color=BLUE_C
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4))
        self.add(counter)

        self.play(n_tracker.animate.set_value(n_flips), run_time=5.0, rate_func=linear)
        running_path.remove_updater(update_path)

        converge_note = Text("Running average → 0.5 as n → ∞", font_size=20, color=GREEN)
        converge_note.to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(Transform(counter, converge_note))
        self.wait(2.5)

    def construct(self):

        self.show_real_number_line()

        self.squish_into_interval()

    def add_weighted_sum(self):
        weighted_sum = MathTex(*it.chain(*[["w_%d"%d, "a_%d"%d, "+"] for d in range(1, 5)] + [["\\cdots", "+", "w_n", "a_n"]]))
        weighted_sum.set_color_by_tex("w_", GREEN)
        weighted_sum.to_edge(UP)
        self.add(weighted_sum)
        self.weighted_sum = weighted_sum

    def show_real_number_line(self):
        weighted_sum = self.weighted_sum
        number_line = NumberLine(x_range=[-3, 3, 1.5])
        number_line.add_numbers()
        number_line.shift(UP)
        arrow1, arrow2 = [Arrow(weighted_sum.get_bottom(), number_line.number_to_point(n)) for n in (-3, 3)]

        self.play(Write(number_line))
        self.play(GrowFromPoint(arrow1, arrow1.get_start()))
        self.play(Transform(arrow1, arrow2, run_time=5, rate_func=there_and_back))
        self.play(FadeOut(arrow1))

        self.number_line = number_line

    def show_interval(self):
        lower_number_line = self.number_line.copy()
        lower_number_line.shift(2*DOWN)
        lower_number_line.set_color(GREY_B)
        lower_number_line.numbers.set_color(WHITE)
        interval = Line(lower_number_line.number_to_point(0), lower_number_line.number_to_point(1), color=YELLOW, stroke_width=5)
        brace = Brace(interval, DOWN, buff=0.7)
        words = Text("Activations should be in this range")
        words.next_to(brace, DOWN, 0.1)

        self.play(ReplacementTransform(self.number_line.copy(), lower_number_line))
        self.play(GrowFromCenter(brace), GrowFromCenter(interval))
        self.play(Write(words, run_time=2))
        self.wait()

        self.lower_number_line = lower_number_line

    def squish_into_interval(self):
        line = self.number_line
        line.remove(*line.numbers)
        ghost_line = line.copy()
        ghost_line.fade(0.5)
        ghost_line.set_color(BLUE_E)
        self.add(ghost_line, line)
        lower_line = self.lower_number_line

        line.generate_target()
        u = line.x_range[2]
        line.target.apply_function(lambda p: np.array([u*expit(p[0])]+list(p[1:])))
        line.target.move_to(lower_line.number_to_point(0.5))

        arrow = Arrow(line.numbers.get_bottom(), line.target.get_top(), color=YELLOW)

        self.play(MoveToTarget(line), GrowFromPoint(arrow, arrow.get_start()))
        self.wait(2)

description: Shows the organization of data into mini-batches and performs a gradient descent step on each.
code:
class OrganizeDataIntoMiniBatches(Scene):
    CONTENT_CENTER = DOWN * 0.5

    def construct(self):
        n_rows = 5
        n_cols = 12
        random.seed(0)
        np.random.seed(0)
        examples = self.get_examples(n_rows, n_cols)
        self.arrange_examples_in_grid(examples, n_rows)
        for example in examples:
            example.save_state()
        alt_order_examples = VGroup(*examples)
        for mob in examples, alt_order_examples:
            random.shuffle(mob.submobjects)
        self.arrange_examples_in_grid(examples, n_rows)

        self.play(LaggedStartMap(
            FadeIn, alt_order_examples,
            lag_ratio=0.2,
            run_time=4
        ))
        self.wait()

        self.examples = examples

        self.shuffle_examples()
        self.divide_into_minibatches(n_rows, n_cols)
        self.one_step_per_batch()

    def shuffle_examples(self):
        self.play(LaggedStartMap(
            Transform, self.examples,
            lambda m: (m.restore,),
            lag_ratio=0.3,
            run_time=3,
            path_arc=np.pi/3,
        ))
        self.wait()

    def divide_into_minibatches(self, n_rows, n_cols):
        examples = self.examples
        examples.sort(lambda p: -p[1])
        rows = VGroup(*[
            VGroup(*examples[i*n_cols:(i+1)*n_cols])
            for i in range(n_rows)
        ])

        mini_batches_words = Text("Mini-batches", font_size=36)
        mini_batches_words.to_edge(UP)
        mini_batches_words.set_color(YELLOW)

        self.play(
            rows.arrange, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT, buff=0.1,
            rows.to_edge, UP, buff=1.5,
            Write(mini_batches_words)
        )

        rects = VGroup(*[
            SurroundingRectangle(
                row, 
                stroke_width=0,
                fill_color=YELLOW,
                fill_opacity=0.25,
            )
            for row in rows
        ])
        self.play(LaggedStartMap(
            FadeIn, rects,
            lag_ratio=0.7,
            rate_func=there_and_back
        ))
        self.wait()

        self.rows = rows
        self.rects = rects

    def one_step_per_batch(self):
        rows = self.rows
        brace = Brace(rows[0], UP, buff=0.1)
        text = brace.get_text(
            "Compute gradient descent step (using backprop)",
            buff=0.1
        )
        def indicate_row(row):
            row.sort(lambda p: p[0])
            return LaggedStartMap(
                ApplyFunction, row,
                lambda row: (
                    lambda m: m.scale(0.75).set_color(YELLOW),
                    row
                ),
                rate_func=wiggle
            )

        self.play(
            FadeOut(mini_batches_words),
            GrowFromCenter(brace),
            Write(text, run_time=2),
        )
        self.play(indicate_row(rows[0]))
        brace.add(text)
        for last_row, row in zip(rows, rows[1:-1]):
            self.play(
                last_row.shift, UP,
                brace.next_to, row, UP, 0.1
            )
            self.play(indicate_row(row))
        self.wait()

    def get_examples(self, n_rows, n_cols):
        n_examples = n_rows * n_cols
        height = 1
        return VGroup(*[
            Square(
                color=BLUE,
                stroke_width=2
            ).set_height(height)
            for _ in range(n_examples)
        ])

    def arrange_examples_in_grid(self, examples, n_rows):
        examples.arrange_in_grid(
            n_rows=n_rows,
            buff=0.1
        )

class BreakApartSum(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        CONTENT_CENTER = DOWN * 0.5
        self.frequencies = [0.5, 1.5, 2, 2.5, 5]
        self.equilibrium_height = 2.0
        self.show_initial_sound()
        self.decompose_sound()
        self.ponder_question()

    def show_initial_sound(self):
        def func(x):
            return self.equilibrium_height + 0.2 * np.sum([
                np.cos(2 * np.pi * f * x) for f in self.frequencies
            ])
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 5, 1],
            y_range=[-1, 5, 1],
            axis_config={"include_tip": False}
        )
        axes.set_width(FRAME_WIDTH - 2)
        axes.set_height(3)
        axes.center()
        axes.to_edge(LEFT)
        graph = always_redraw(lambda: axes.plot(func, color=YELLOW))

        v_line = always_redraw(lambda: Line(ORIGIN, 4 * UP).move_to(axes.i2gp(0, graph), DOWN))
        dot = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(color=PINK).move_to(graph.point_from_function(0)))

        self.add(axes, graph, v_line, dot)
        self.play(
            v_line.move_to, axes.coords_to_point(5, 0), DOWN,
            MoveAlongPath(dot, graph),
            run_time=8,
            rate_func=linear,
        )
        self.play(*map(FadeOut, [dot, v_line]))
        self.set_variables_as_attrs(axes, graph)

    def decompose_sound(self):
        axes, graph = self.axes, self.graph

        pure_graphs = VGroup(*[
            always_redraw(lambda freq=f: axes.plot(
                lambda x: 0.5 * np.cos(2 * np.pi * freq * x),
                color=[BLUE, RED][i % 2],
            ))
            for i, freq in enumerate(self.frequencies)
        ])
        pure_graphs.arrange(DOWN, buff=0.5)
        h_line = DashedLine(6 * LEFT, 6 * RIGHT)

        self.play(FadeOut(axes), graph.to_edge, UP)
        pure_graphs.next_to(graph, DOWN, 0.75)
        h_line.next_to(graph, DOWN, 0.5)
        self.play(Create(h_line))
        for pure_graph in reversed(pure_graphs):
            self.play(ReplacementTransform(graph.copy(), pure_graph))
        self.wait()

        self.all_graphs = VGroup(graph, h_line, pure_graphs)
        self.pure_graphs = pure_graphs

    def ponder_question(self):
        all_graphs = self.all_graphs
        pure_graphs = self.pure_graphs
        dot = Dot(point=CONTENT_CENTER, color=PINK)
        self.add(dot)
        self.play(
            all_graphs.scale, 0.75,
            all_graphs.to_corner, UP+RIGHT,
        )
        rect = SurroundingRectangle(pure_graphs, color=WHITE)
        self.play(
            Create(rect),
            LaggedStartMap(
                lambda g: g.animate.shift(0.1 * UP).set_color(YELLOW),
                pure_graphs,
                rate_func=lambda t: wiggle(t)  # Using built-in Sinusoidal
            )
        )
        self.play(FadeOut(rect))
        self.wait()

class WrapCosineGraphAroundCircle(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        CONTENT_CENTER = DOWN * 0.5
        self.initial_winding_frequency = 0.5
        self.signal_frequency = 3.0
        self.show_initial_signal()
        self.show_finite_interval()
        self.wrap_around_circle()
        self.show_time_sweeps()
        self.compare_two_frequencies()
        self.change_wrapping_frequency()

    def show_initial_signal(self):
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 5, 1],
            y_range=[-2, 2, 1],
            axis_config={"include_tip": False}
        )
        graph = always_redraw(lambda: axes.plot(
            lambda x: 0.5 * np.cos(2 * np.pi * self.signal_frequency * x),
            color=YELLOW
        ))
        self.graph = graph
        braces = VGroup(*[Brace(graph, DOWN, buff=0.1) for _ in range(3)])

        v_lines = VGroup(*[
            DashedLine(ORIGIN, 2 * UP, color=RED).move_to(axes.coords_to_point(i, 0))
            for i in range(1, 3)
        ])
        words = Text("Beats per second", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)

        self.add(axes)
        self.play(Create(graph, rate_func=linear))
        self.play(FadeIn(words), LaggedStartMap(FadeIn, braces), *map(Create, v_lines))
        self.wait()
        self.play(FadeOut(VGroup(braces, v_lines)), words.to_edge, UP)
        self.wait()

        self.beats_per_second_label = words
        self.graph = graph

    def show_finite_interval(self):
        axes = self.time_axes
        v_line = DashedLine(axes.coords_to_point(0, 0), axes.coords_to_point(0, axes.y_range[1]), color=RED)
        h_line = Line(axes.coords_to_point(0, 0), axes.coords_to_point(axes.x_range[1], 0))
        rect = Rectangle(stroke_width=0, fill_color=TEAL, fill_opacity=0.5)
        rect.match_height(v_line)
        rect.match_width(h_line, stretch=True)
        rect.move_to(v_line, DOWN + LEFT)

        right_v_line = v_line.copy().move_to(rect, RIGHT)
        rect.save_state()
        rect.stretch(0, 0, about_edge=ORIGIN)
        self.play(rect.restore, run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeOut(rect))
        for line in v_line, right_v_line:
            self.play(Create(line))
            self.play(FadeOut(line))
        self.wait()

    def wrap_around_circle(self):
        graph = self.graph
        circle_plane = self.get_circle_plane()
        moving_graph = graph.copy()

        self.play(Create(circle_plane, lag_ratio=0))
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(
            moving_graph,
            graph,
            run_time=3,
            path_arc=-TAU / 2
        ))
        self.animate_frequency_change([graph], self.initial_winding_frequency)
        self.wait()
        pg_copy = self.get_polarized_mobject(graph, freq=self.initial_winding_frequency)
        self.remove(graph)
        self.play(pg_copy.set_opacity, 0.25)
        self.play(*self.get_vector_animations(graph), run_time=15)
        self.remove(pg_copy)
        self.wait()

    def show_time_sweeps(self):
        freq = self.initial_winding_frequency
        graph = self.graph

        v_lines = self.get_v_lines_indicating_periods(freq)
        winding_freq_label = Text(f"Winding frequency: {freq}", font_size=34).next_to(v_lines, UP)

        self.animate_time_sweep(freq=freq, t_max=4, run_time=6, added_anims=[FadeIn(v_lines)])
        self.play(
            FadeIn(winding_freq_label),
            *self.get_vector_animations(graph)
        )
        self.wait()

        self.v_lines_indicating_periods = v_lines

    def compare_two_frequencies(self):
        bps_label = self.beats_per_second_label
        wps_label = self.winding_freq_label
        for label in (bps_label, wps_label):
            label.rect = SurroundingRectangle(label, color=RED)

        graph = self.graph
        freq = self.initial_winding_frequency
        braces = self.get_peak_braces(buff=0)

        self.play(Create(bps_label.rect))
        self.play(FadeOut(bps_label.rect))
        self.play(LaggedStartMap(FadeIn, braces, run_time=3))
        self.play(FadeOut(braces))
        self.play(Create(wps_label.rect))
        self.play(FadeOut(wps_label.rect))
        self.animate_time_sweep(freq=freq, t_max=4)
        self.wait()

    def change_wrapping_frequency(self):
        graph = self.graph
        v_lines = self.v_lines_indicating_periods
        freq_label = self.winding_freq_label

        for target_freq in [1.23, 0.2, 0.79, 1.55, self.signal_frequency]:
            self.play(
                Transform(
                    v_lines,
                    self.get_v_lines_indicating_periods(target_freq)
                )
            )
            self.play(*self.get_vector_animations(graph, freq=target_freq))
            self.wait()

description: Simulates Brownian motion with small particles colliding with a larger particle and each other, showing the derived path of the large particle.
code:
class BrownianMotion(Scene):
    CONTENT_CENTER = DOWN * 0.5

    def construct(self):
        self.add_title()
        self.add_particles()
        self.wait(60)

    def add_title(self):
        square = Square(side_length=6)
        title = Text("Brownian motion", font_size=34).scale(1.5)
        title.next_to(square, UP)

        self.add(square)
        self.add(title)

    def add_particles(self):
        m1, m2 = 1, 100
        r1, r2 = 0.05, 0.5
        L, max_v = 3, 5
        n_particles = 100

        lil_particles = VGroup(*[
            self.get_particle(m1, r1, L, max_v)
            for _ in range(n_particles)
        ])
        big_particle = self.get_particle(m2, r2, L=r2, max_v=0)
        big_particle.set_fill(YELLOW, 1)

        for p in lil_particles:
            if self.are_colliding(p, big_particle):
                lil_particles.remove(p)
        all_particles = VGroup(big_particle, *lil_particles)
        all_particles.add_updater(self.update_particles)

        path = self.get_traced_path(big_particle)

        self.add(all_particles)
        self.add(path)

        self.particles = all_particles
        self.big_particle = big_particle
        self.path = path

    def get_particle(self, m, r, L, max_v):
        dot = Dot(radius=r)
        dot.set_fill(WHITE, 0.7)
        dot.mass = m
        dot.radius = r
        dot.center = np.add(
            np.random.uniform(-L + r, L - r) * RIGHT,
            np.random.uniform(-L + r, L - r) * UP
        )
        dot.move_to(dot.center)
        dot.velocity = rotate_vector(
            np.random.uniform(0, max_v) * RIGHT,
            np.random.uniform(0, TAU),
        )
        return dot

    def are_colliding(self, p1, p2):
        d = get_norm(p1.get_center() - p2.get_center())
        return (d < p1.radius + p2.radius)

    def get_traced_path(self, particle):
        path = VMobject()
        path.set_stroke(BLUE, 3)
        path.start_new_path(particle.get_center())

        buff = 0.02

        def update_path(path):
            new_point = particle.get_center()
            if get_norm(new_point - path.get_last_point()) > buff:
                path.add_line_to(new_point)

        path.add_updater(update_path)
        return path

    def update_particles(self, particles, dt):
        for p1 in particles:
            p1.center += p1.velocity * dt

            # Check particle collisions
            buff = 0.01
            for p2 in particles:
                if p1 is p2:
                    continue
                v = p2.center - p1.center
                dist = get_norm(v)
                r_sum = p1.radius + p2.radius
                diff = dist - r_sum
                if diff < 0:
                    unit_v = v / dist
                    p1.center += (diff - buff) * unit_v / 2
                    p2.center += -(diff - buff) * unit_v / 2
                    u1 = p1.velocity
                    u2 = p2.velocity
                    m1 = p1.mass
                    m2 = p2.mass
                    v1 = (
                        (m2 * (u2 - u1) + m1 * u1 + m2 * u2) /
                        (m1 + m2)
                    )
                    v2 = (
                        (m1 * (u1 - u2) + m1 * u1 + m2 * u2) /
                        (m1 + m2)
                    )
                    p1.velocity = v1
                    p2.velocity = v2

            # Check edge collisions
            r1 = p1.radius
            c1 = p1.center
            for i in [0, 1]:
                if abs(c1[i]) + r1 > 3:
                    c1[i] = np.sign(c1[i]) * (3 - r1)
                    p1.velocity[i] *= -1 * np.sign(p1.velocity[i]) * np.sign(c1[i])

        for p in particles:
            p.move_to(p.center)
        return particles

class ComplexMappingScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        CONTENT_CENTER = DOWN * 0.5
        title = Text('Complex Plane', font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.add(title)

        plane = ComplexPlane()
        plane.add_coordinates()
        plane.coordinate_labels.submobjects.pop(-1)
        self.add(plane)

        unit_circle = Circle(
            radius=plane.x_range[2],

        )

        dot = Dot()

        exp_tex = MathTex('e^{', '0.00', 'i}').to_corner(UR)
        zero = exp_tex.get_part_by_tex('0.00')
        zero.fade(1)

        exp_decimal = DecimalNumber(0, num_decimal_places=2, color=YELLOW)
        exp_decimal.move_to(zero)

        def update_exp_decimal(exp_dec):
            exp_dec.set_value(TAU)
            exp_dec.move_to(zero)

        exp_decimal.add_updater(update_exp_decimal)

        self.play(
            Create(unit_circle, run_time=5),
            Write(exp_tex),
            dot.animate.move_to(unit_circle.point_from_proportion(0.25)),
            UpdateFromAlphaFunc(exp_decimal, lambda ed, a: ed.set_fill(opacity=a))
        )
        self.play(
            FadeOut(exp_tex),
            FadeOut(exp_decimal),
            FadeOut(dot),
            unit_circle.set_fill(BLACK, opacity=1)
        )
        self.wait()

description: Applies the Joukowsky map to a warped grid and streamlines, demonstrating complex function transformations.
code:
class JoukowskyMapScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        CONTENT_CENTER = DOWN * 0.5
        title = Text('Joukowsky Map', font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        self.add(title)

        plane = ComplexPlane()
        plane.add_coordinates()
        self.add(plane)

        unit_circle = Circle(
            radius=plane.x_range[2],
            stroke_color=YELLOW,
            fill_color=BLACK,
            fill_opacity=0
        )
        self.add(unit_circle)

        grid = NumberPlane()
        grid.prepare_for_nonlinear_transform()

        self.play(
            grid.apply_function, lambda p: inverse_joukowsky_map(p)
        )

        vector_field_func = lambda p: np.array([np.cos(p[1]), np.sin(p[0]), 0])
        stream_lines = StreamLines(vector_field_func, virtual_time=3, min_magnitude=0, max_magnitude=2)
        self.add(stream_lines)

        self.play(ShowCreationThenDestruction(stream_lines), run_time=5)

        self.play(grid.apply_function, lambda p: joukowsky_map(p), run_time=3)

        self.wait()

description: This scene compares probabilities of stereotypes using value tracking and probability bars.
code:
class DescriptionOfSteve(Scene):
    CONTENT_CENTER = DOWN * 0.5

    def construct(self):
        self.write_description()
        self.compare_probabilities()

    def write_description(self):
        steve = Dot(radius=0.4).to_corner(UR)
        description = self.get_description()
        description.to_edge(LEFT)
        description.align_to(steve, UP)

        mt_parts = VGroup(
            description.get_part_by_tex("meek"),
            description.get_part_by_tex("soul"),
        )
        mt_parts.set_color(WHITE)

        self.add(steve)
        self.play(
            FadeIn(description),
            run_time=3,
            lag_ratio=0.01,
            rate_func=linear,
        )
        self.wait(3)

        lines = VGroup(*[
            Line(mob.get_corner(DL), mob.get_corner(DR), color=YELLOW)
            for mob in mt_parts
        ])
        self.play(
            Create(lines),
            mt_parts.set_color, YELLOW,
        )
        self.play(FadeOut(lines))
        self.wait()

    def compare_probabilities(self):
        p_tracker = ValueTracker(0.5)
        rect = Rectangle(width=10, height=1).move_to(1.75 * DOWN)
        rect.add_updater(lambda m: m.set_fill(opacity=p_tracker.get_value()))
        icons = VGroup(
            Triangle().set_height(0.7).next_to(rect,LEFT),
            Circle().set_height(0.7).next_to(rect,RIGHT)
        )
        bar_icons = VGroup(rect, *icons)
        bar_icons.set_opacity(0)

        q_marks = MathTex("???")
        q_marks.scale(1.5)
        q_marks.space_out_submobjects(1.5)
        q_marks.next_to(bar_icons, DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(bar_icons))
        self.wait()
        self.play(
            p_tracker.animate.set_value(0.9),
            icons[0].set_opacity, 1,
        )
        self.wait()
        self.play(
            p_tracker.animate.set_value(0.1),
            icons[1].set_opacity, 1,
        )

        for value in [0.3, 0.7]:
            self.play(
                p_tracker.animate.set_value(value),
                run_time=7,
            )
        self.play(
            LaggedStartMap(
                FadeIn, q_marks,
                lambda m: (m, UP),
                run_time=2,
            )
        )

    def get_description(self):
        return MathTex(
            """
            Steve is very shy and withdrawn,\\
            invariably helpful but with very\\
            little interest in people or in the world of reality.\\
            A meek and tidy soul, he has a need for order\\
            and structure, and a passion for detail.\\
            """,
            tex_to_color_map={
                "shy and withdrawn": BLUE,
                "meek and tidy": YELLOW,
                "soul": YELLOW,
            },
            alignment="",
        )

description: This scene explores the composition and correction view of farmers and librarians through rearranging elements.
code:
class CorrectViewOfFarmersAndLibrarians(Scene):
    CONTENT_CENTER = DOWN * 0.5

    def construct(self):
        book = Dot(radius=0.4).set_height(1).move_to(DOWN + 2 * LEFT)
        farm = Dot(radius=0.4).set_height(1).move_to(DOWN + 2 * LEFT)
        farm.shift(4 * RIGHT)

        steve_loc = UP + 2 * LEFT
        steves = VGroup(book.copy(), farm.copy())
        steves.move_to(steve_loc)

        self.add(steves, book, farm)
        arrows = VGroup(
            Arrow(steve.get_bottom(), icon.get_top())
            for steve, icon in zip(steves, [book, farm])
        )
        words = VGroup(
            Text("Stereotype"),
            Text("Unexpected"),
        )
        for arrow, word, vect in zip(arrows, words, [LEFT, RIGHT]):
            word.scale(1.5)
            word.next_to(arrow, vect)
            self.play(
                Create(arrow),
                FadeIn(word, UP),
            )
        self.wait()

        librarian = Dot(radius=0.4).to_edge(UP, LEFT)
        farmer = Dot(radius=0.4).to_edge(UP, RIGHT)

        farmers = VGroup(farmer, *[farmer.copy() for _ in range(19)])
        farmers.arrange_in_grid(4, 5, buff=0.1)
        farmers.move_to(farmer.get_corner(UL), UL)

        farmer_outlines = farmers.copy()
        farmer_outlines.set_fill(opacity=0)
        farmer_outlines.set_stroke(YELLOW, 1)

        farmer_count = Integer(1)
        farmer_count.scale(2)
        farmer_count.set_color(GREEN)
        farmer_count.next_to(farmers, LEFT, buff=0.75)
        farmer_count.add_updater(lambda m: m.set_value(len(farmer_outlines)))

        for person, icon in zip([librarian, farmer], [book, farm]):
            person.save_state()
            person.set_opacity(0)
            person.move_to(icon, DR)
        self.remove(book, farm)

        self.play(
            LaggedStartMap(FadeOut, VGroup(steves, arrows, words)),
            FadeIn(librarian),
            FadeIn(farmer),
            run_time=1,
        )
        self.play(LaggedStartMap(FadeIn, farmers[1:]))
        self.wait()

        self.add(farmer_count)
        self.play(
            ShowIncreasingSubsets(farmer_outlines),
            run_time=2,
        )
        self.play(FadeOut(farmer_outlines))
        self.wait()

        farmers.generate_target()
        farmers.target.scale(0.5, about_edge=UL)

        new_farmers = VGroup(*[
            farmers.target.copy().next_to(
                farmers.target, dir, buff=0.1
            )
            for dir in [RIGHT, DOWN]
        ])
        new_farmers[-10:].align_to(new_farmers[0], RIGHT)
        new_farmers[-10:].align_to(new_farmers[-11], UP)

        farmer_count.clear_updaters()
        self.play(
            MoveToTarget(farmers),
            ShowIncreasingSubsets(new_farmers),
            farmer_count.animate.set_value(60),
        )
        self.wait()
        self.play(
            FadeOut(new_farmers),
            Restore(farmers),
            farmer_count.animate.set_value(20),
        )
        self.wait()
        farmers.generate_target()
        librarian.generate_target()
        group = VGroup(librarian.target, *farmers.target)
        group.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        group.set_width(FRAME_WIDTH - 3)
        group.to_edge(DOWN)

        l_brace = Brace(librarian.target, UP, buff=0.1)
        f_brace = Brace(farmers.target, UP, buff=0.1)

        self.play(
            MoveToTarget(librarian),
            MoveToTarget(farmers),
            ReplacementTransform(farmer_count, f_brace),
            GrowFromCenter(f_brace),
        )
        self.play(GrowFromCenter(l_brace))
        self.wait()
